channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Litter on Tabard street, 22nd July
item.description:
There are plastic bottles, food waste/packaging, coffee cups, tissues and cans on the pavement and road on Tabard street. It has been there two days.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tabard Street, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bin left in street, now overflowing, 22nd July
item.description:
Bin has been left in street and is now overflowing, buzzing with flies. Next collection is only Wednesday next week. Could be related to no 7 Chatterton Road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chatterton Road, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside 121 Walm Lane NW2, 22nd July
item.description:
Approx 15 large bin bags full of trash

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Walm Lane, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load

item.title: Bin bags, 22nd July
item.description:
More bin bags

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lucey Way, Bermondsey, Greater London

item.title: Drain overflowing, 22nd July
item.description:
The drain has been overflowing for over a week. Water all over the pavement.can be slippery

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beverley Road, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Further to report 9825830, 22nd July
item.description:
Further to previous report - the fly tipped bags are full of butchered meat among other things - so far Chicken has been identified as their legs as sticking out of the black bag - there are mutliple flies and I would think the foxes are going to have a field day this evening. Certainly a health hazard
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Linkfield, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light not working, 22nd July
item.description:
The street light on the edge of the car park on Montana Gardens is not working for the last few nights. Opposite number 10. Please can this be fixed asap as it's very dark at night and it's a cut through for Meadowview road residents walking home from the station.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Montana Gardens, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: On the pavement l, 22nd July
item.description:
Someone has dumped and raided some suitcases and left all the leftovers on the floor

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Walm Lane, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: Tags over mural at side of M&S Food, 22nd July
item.description:
Growing number of tags on mural. Could these be cleaned off. Many thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 446-450, Brixton Road, Brixton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lots of junk dumped at end of Harbour Road, 22nd July
item.description:
Put there a couple of days ago. Thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 35, Southwell Road, Loughborough Junction, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The entire front of the Station needs sweeping and then jet cleaning., 22nd July
item.description:
As a regular user of Finsbury Park station for well over 15 years I am well aware of how dirty and disgusting the whole of the Station Place has become.There are discarded fast food containers, litter and cigarette butts all over the floor.The road surface and pavements are sticky,dirty and smelly.It is very depressing to have to wait here for a bus.Thousands of people use this station and have to experience the utter neglect of it and what it is saying to them...' Hackney Council,Islington Council and London Transport don't really care about the people who travel through here.' I have never seen any council worker sweeping up or any council operative using any mechanical help to clean the area.It has the potential to being an attractive area if more thought and care were put into it.Surely it wouldn't cost much money to have a dedicated team of cleaners to keep the area fresh and clean.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 4-5, Station Place, Finsbury Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Food Waste Bins, 22nd July
item.description:
I previously reported food waste bins outside 114 Greenwood Road that had old rubbish which hadn't been removed (Fix my Steeet ref 9803036). The remaining bins have broken lids and 1 is full of rubbish not in compostible bags so they won't be emptied.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Greenwood Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on wall at corner of eastlake road, 22nd July
item.description:
Vost tag on wall. Thank

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 148, Coldharbour Lane, Loughborough Junction, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Contradicting parking sign, 22nd July
item.description:
A new sign was recently installed alongside the parking bay on Edward Rd (on the end, at the junction with Green Lane) advising that these bays can be used by residents with the CPZ permit as well as for those who pay. However, the previous/existing sign (alongside the controlled zone entry sign) remains in place which states that these bays are paid only
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Edward Road, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytip, 22nd July
item.description:
Large white door left on pavement near bus stop.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Croydon Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Shrubbery overhanging the pavement, 22nd July
item.description:
Has been reported numerous times with no resolution. The vegetation is impacting people with children / pushchairs and also wheelchairs moving along the pavement.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): The Pantiles, Upper Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside 9 lyndhurst road, 22nd July
item.description:
Tree massively overhang boundary line- almost touching building. Blocking light from ground floor and basement flat only front facing windows and branches falling into front garden causing harm to dogs

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 6, Lyndhurst Road, Belsize Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Murray Avenue, Widmore, Greater London
